The Legend of Tarzan (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $126,643,061Details
International Box Office $230,600,000
Worldwide Box Office $357,243,061
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $12,612,717
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $13,073,114
Total $25,685,831
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It has been years since the man once known as Tarzan left the jungles of Africa behind for a gentrified life as John Clayton III, Lord Greystoke, with his beloved wife, Jane at his side. Now, he has been invited back to the Congo to serve as a trade emissary of Parliament, unaware that he is a pawn in a deadly convergence of greed and revenge, masterminded by the Belgian, Captain Leon Rom. But those behind the murderous plot have no idea what they are about to unleash.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$38,527,856 (30.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.29 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$180,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,561 opening theaters/3,591 max. theaters, 4.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $175,104,159

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 1st, 2016 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
July 1st, 2016 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: June 27th, 2016 (Wide) (South Korea)
July 1st, 2016 (Wide), released as Legend of Tarzan, The (New Zealand)
July 1st, 2016 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
July 1st, 2016 (Wide) (Poland)
July 7th, 2016 (Wide), released as Tarzanas: džiunglių legenda (Lithuania)
Video Release: September 23rd, 2016 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of action and violence, some sensuality and brief rude dialogue.
(Rating bulletin 2417 (Cert #50329), 3/23/2016)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Motion Capture Performance
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Africa
Plot point: Government Corruption, Orphan, Romance, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Village Roadshow Productions, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment, Jerry Weintraub, Riche-Ludwig, Beaglepug
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

It has been years since the man once known as Tarzan left the jungles of Africa behind for a gentrified life as John Clayton III, Lord Greystoke, with his beloved wife, Jane at his side. Now, he has been invited back to the Congo to serve as a trade emissary of Parliament, unaware that he is a pawn in a deadly convergence of greed and revenge, masterminded by the Belgian, Captain Leon Rom. But those behind the murderous plot have no idea what they are about to unleash.
